features.summary: Costco reported Q2 fiscal 2021 net income of $951 million ($2.14 per share) versus $931 million ($2.10 per share) last year. Net sales increased 14.7% to $43.89 billion with comparable sales up 13.0% (12.9% excluding gas deflation and FX). E-commerce sales surged 75.8% year-over-year. The company incurred $246 million in COVID-related premium wage costs during the quarter. Gross margin declined 2 basis points to 10.96%, primarily due to lower ancillary business margins from gas operations, partially offset by strong core merchandise margins up 71 basis points.
features.management_outlook: Management discontinued the $2/hour COVID premium pay effective February 28 but implemented permanent wage increases of $1/hour for starting wages and $0.45/hour for top-scale workers. They expect COVID-related expenses to decrease by over half going forward, from the $1.6 billion spent over the past 12 months. The company plans to open 13 new warehouses in the second half of fiscal 2021 and maintains CapEx guidance of $3.0-3.2 billion. Management expressed confidence in maintaining competitive pricing and continuing expansion of Kirkland Signature products and vertical integration initiatives.
features.risks: Supply chain challenges persist with container shortages and port delays affecting furniture, sporting goods, and food items. Component shortages are impacting electronics, exercise equipment, and appliances. Gas margin volatility remains unpredictable and significantly impacts profitability. The company faces difficult year-over-year comparisons in upcoming quarters due to strong pandemic-driven sales in 2020. Fresh food margin improvements may not be sustainable as sales growth normalizes, potentially reducing labor productivity gains and spoilage benefits.
features.key_drivers: Strong core merchandise performance drove 71 basis points of margin improvement, led by fresh foods benefiting from higher labor productivity and lower spoilage. E-commerce growth of 75.8% is approaching 10% of total business with continued investment in fulfillment capabilities. Membership renewal rates improved to 91.0% in US/Canada and 88.5% worldwide, supported by executive membership upgrades and credit card adoption. The permanent wage increases are expected to improve employee retention and productivity while positioning Costco competitively in the labor market.
